provenance.snippet: Scope: Cold/parallel migration from legacy mx02 (RHEL 5.7) to new KVM VM with Storinator-backed mail data.
This repository is the working migration package for moving mail services from legacy `mx02` to a modern target stack while preserving user access continuity.
- Preserve mailbox data for all in-scope domains.
- Preserve authentication so users can continue using existing passwords.
- Execute controlled cutover with rollback readiness.
- Keep evidence, runbooks, and decision history in version control.
- `runbooks/`: discovery, build/migrate, and cutover execution steps.
- Added explicit export scope and acceptance gates for password continuity.
- Added runbook command blocks for MySQL auth export/import and post-import validation in `runbooks/04_BUILD_AND_MIGRATE_RUNBOOK.md`.
- Added cutover checklist controls for auth parity and existing-password login validation in `runbooks/05_CUTOVER_CHECKLIST.md`.
- Added repository-level `README.md` with migration goals, scope, data requirements, and acceptance criteria.
- Updated approved plan to include mandatory export of:
- Updated plan and runbooks to enforce that users should log in with existing passwords after migration.
- Auth backend on `mx02` verified as MySQL via `/home/vpopmail/etc/vpopmail.mysql`.
- Mailbox export progress and health checks are being monitored from `hoth` against `/Backups-Pool/Backups-Volume/mx02_backup`.
Capture exact legacy role, data paths, auth model, and service behavior.
- Mail role: relay / full mailbox / filter+relay
- Auth backend: local / LDAP / AD / SQL
- Actual mailbox path and quota model
- Initial sizing: 8-16 vCPU, 16-32 GB RAM (adjust by observed concurrency)
- Full mailbox server: Postfix + Dovecot + Rspamd + DB backend (if virtual users)
- domains, aliases, users/auth, transport/relay policy, spam policy, TLS, quotas
## Export auth and aliases from MX02 (MySQL-backed vpopmail)
- [ ] Validate SMTP in/out, IMAP/POP, TLS, quotas, spam policy
- [ ] Snapshot VM + storage + config backup + DB dump
- [ ] Export vpopmail MySQL auth tables for all cutover domains
- [ ] Export `valias` table and required vpopmail metadata tables
- [ ] Record source row counts per exported table (`row_counts_source.txt`)
- [ ] Freeze writes on source or relay source->target
- [ ] Import MySQL auth tables on target (`pw_passwd` hash-preserving)
- [ ] Import `valias` and supporting vpopmail tables on target
- [ ] Verify source vs target row-count parity for domain auth tables
- [ ] Execute login checks with existing passwords for sample users in each domain
- [ ] Switch MX / relay / NAT / firewall flows
- [ ] Start production traffic on new VM
- [ ] Monitor queue depth, auth failures, deferred mail, IMAP errors
- UID/GID mismatch on migrated mailbox trees
- Hidden legacy policy behavior not migrated logically
- SMTP rate and deferred queue behavior
1. Add persistent `/etc/fstab` entry on mx02 for `nfs4` mount (if desired for continuity).
2. Confirm backup job path now points to `/mx02_backup` and performs real remote writes.
3. Add monitoring/alert for mount availability and stale NFS handles.
1. Execute discovery runbook on legacy mail source.
2. Build new KVM VM with split OS/data disks.
3. Implement staged mailbox migration and pilot.
4. Execute controlled cutover checklist with rollback window.
- Keep rollback mapping document updated with exact MX/NAT/relay reversal steps.
Keep OS/spool separate from mailbox content.
- Queue/spool on remote/shared storage is high-risk.
- Mailbox content can safely live on large Storinator-backed volume.
- Storinator -> KVM host storage -> dedicated VM virtual disk -> filesystem in VM
Avoid direct fragile app-to-network-share designs for active queue/spool.
- Postfix: SMTP ingress/egress, policy and routing
- Distinct relay policy for trusted/internal senders
- Quota and mailbox ownership model validated before production
# Live Network and NFS Fix Log (Session)
NFS path issue was resolved through context-correct firewall policy and export authorization fix.
1. Initial ACL work was applied in wrong ASA context (`admin`) and did not affect mx02 data path.
2. Context validation found real gateway path for mx02 at `SYD-DC-TEIR1` (`192.168.145.33`).
3. Added correct ACL permit in `SYD-DC-TEIR1` on `apnt1dmz03-out` for `tcp/2049` from `192.168.145.36` to `172.16.254.6`.
4. On hoth, tcpdump confirmed packets reached NFS service; NFSv4 replied with `Permission denied` at getattr.
5. Root cause on hoth export: `root_squash` for mx02 export entry.
6. Changed only mx02 export entry to `no_root_squash`, reloaded exports.
7. Final validation from mx02: `mount -t nfs4` successful; read/write/delete successful on mounted export.
- Removed only test artifacts in wrong admin context.
- `tcp/2049` reachable from mx02 after TEIR1 ACL fix.
- `nfs4` mount succeeded after hoth export fix.
On this old client stack, use `-t nfs4` (not `-o vers=4`).
